eta_crit.note: strict_compliant_tps is decreasing, so its max over [0, eta_ceil] is at eta=0 (= deployed 481.53 < 500); no eta in [0, eta_ceil] reaches 500. The analytic crossing is at NEGATIVE eta (a speedup), confirming closure.

cross_checks.note: the (1-eta) linear FORM round-trips both of #326's banked ceilings bit-exactly; the #213 EXACT divisor map round-trips 500. Near the realistic floor the linear/divisor gap is only -0.04 TPS, so the closure is robust under both forms; near the off-the-shelf ceil the gap widens to -36.2 TPS (still both << 500).

synthesis.verdict: NECESSARY-BUT-NOT-SUFFICIENT. Under the #319 strict-lock the custom reduction-invariant kernel lifts the strict-compliant frontier from 165.44 TPS (non-spec int4 M=1 AR, #196) to 476.98 TPS at its first-principles floor eta=0.0095 (#216) -- a +311.5 TPS lift that restores almost all of the spec premium. But the deployed base 481.53 < 500, so kernel_alone_clears_500=False and eta_crit_500=None (would need eta=-0.0384 < 0, i.e. a ~3.8% SPEEDUP, not an overhead). The frontier stalls 23.0 TPS short of 500. The custom kernel is the ONLY thing that makes the fast spec path compliant, but a FASTER substrate UNDER it (sub-int4 body -- lawine's companion card) is required to close the residual ~23 TPS. Off-the-shelf VBI (eta=0.3141) is far worse: 330.28 TPS. 0 GPU, 0 TPS, baseline 481.53 UNCHANGED. Bank-the-analysis.
synthesis.handoff: the custom reduction-invariant kernel is NECESSARY (only route to a compliant fast path; lifts 165.44 -> 477 TPS, +312) but NOT SUFFICIENT alone (481.53 < 500 -> stalls 23 TPS short, eta_crit_500=None). The next lever is a sub-int4 body UNDER the kernel (lawine's companion card) to lift the 482 base above 500 before the (1-eta) kernel haircut.

scope: CPU-only analytic over banked merged *_results.json (#196 laxllfjl, #326 io4cs2ch, #213 5o7zcj8s). NO model build / training / served-file change / HF Job / submission. 0 GPU, 0 TPS. BASELINE 481.53 UNCHANGED. Bank-the-analysis.
